headline: Meet a hard moment with self-compassion
subtext: Three steps to steady yourself when symptoms flare.
steps: Name it: this is a hard moment.
Normalise it: hard moments are part of being human.
Offer yourself the kindness you would give a friend.
caption:
When symptoms flare, the harshest voice in the room is often our own. Self-criticism keeps the nervous system on high alert, which is exactly the wrong direction for recovery.

The self-compassion break is a simple reset. You acknowledge that this is a hard moment, remind yourself that struggle is part of being human, and offer yourself a few kind words, the way you would to a friend. It eases the stress response that amplifies symptoms.

For chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, kindness is not soft, it is regulation.

More practices at https://neurowaze.health

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Self-compassion practices are shown to lower the body's stress response and are widely used in chronic-pain recovery. Find guided versions in the Neurowaze Resources area: https://neurowaze.health
